mapping.a_element: a geometric representation of the object (a hyperbolic embedding of the graph)
mapping.b_element: a combinatorial representation of the object (a simplicial complex built from measurements)

brief_justification: The only overlap is the vague theme of assigning a geometric/topological representation to an underlying structure; the actual machinery differs. Paper A performs a metric hyperbolic embedding of a scale-free network to achieve lossless compression, whereas paper B uses projections in an observer's von Neumann algebra to assemble a simplicial complex that captures the topology of spacetime — a metric-embedding optimization versus an algebraic-measurement topological construction, with no role-for-role correspondence of driving equations, thresholds, or processes.
